data.title: GDC IT Solutions Announces Strategic Asset Acquisition of Global Help Desk Services, Inc., Expanding 100% U.S.-Based Outsourced IT Service Desk Capabilities
data.description: GDC IT Solutions, based in Chambersburg, Pennsylvania, recently announced the acquisition of Global Help Desk Services, Inc. (GHDSi). This move marks an expansion in their IT service desk capabilities, now offering 24/7 support internationally. The acquisition brings together two reputable companies to enhance their services and reach a broader client base.

data.title: AI agents OpenClaw and Manus Desktop compete in 2026. Which one suits your needs? Read more to make an informed choice.
data.description: In 2026, OpenClaw's open-source framework is competing with Manus Desktop's polished automation suite in the AI market. Users are urged to consider which AI agent best aligns with their workflow needs.

data.title: IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm to Shape the Future of Enterprise Computing
data.description: Armonk, New York - In a move to enhance infrastructure options and ensure the stability of crucial operations, IBM revealed a partnership with Arm on April 2, 2026. This collaboration, aimed at creating innovative dual-architecture hardware, signifies a significant step towards technological advancements. IBM's stock, traded as IBM on NYSE, is expected to gain traction from this strategic initiative.

data.title: Kyndryl launches Agentic Service Management to power AI-native infrastructure services and intelligent workflows
data.description: In New York today, Kyndryl (NYSE: KD), a top provider of crucial enterprise technology services, unveiled its Agentic Service Management. This innovative offering integrates a maturity model, structured evaluations, and implementation strategies to assist enterprises in enhancing their operational efficiency.

data.title: Alpha Ladder Group and MetaComp Partner with Maqam International Holding, an Abu Dhabi (UAE) company, to Advance RWA Tokenisation and Web2.5 Payments Across Singapore-UAE Corridor
data.description: On April 2, 2026, Alpha Ladder Group, based in Singapore, announced its strategic focus on sustainable financial and technology innovation. This effort is driven through its subsidiaries, MetaComp and Alpha Ladder Finance. Maqam International Holding, a company based in Abu Dhabi, is also involved in this initiative. The collaboration between these two entities is aimed at enhancing their presence in the market and furthering their innovative solutions.

data.title: South Korean firm LIG Nex1's missile interceptors shine in Iran war, costing much less than U.S. interceptors.
data.description: Reports indicate that missile interceptors produced by the South Korean company LIG Nex1 have been demonstrating strong performance. These interceptors are reportedly being manufactured at a significantly lower cost compared to their U.S. counterparts. The interceptors from LIG Nex1 are proving to be effective and efficient, offering a cost-effective solution for missile defense. The technology created by the South Korean firm is garnering attention for its quality and affordability, with costs coming in at a fraction of what U.S. interceptors typically entail. This development showcases how these interceptors are not only reliable but also economically viable in the realm of missile defense systems.

data.title: Caliway Selected to Present Preclinical Data on CBL-514 in Combination with GLP-1R Therapy at the American Diabetes Association's 2026 Scientific Sessions
data.description: A study involving Caliway's experimental fat reduction drug CBL-514 in conjunction with GLP-1R weight-loss treatments has been chosen for formal presentation at the American Diabetes Association's 2026 Scientific Sessions. The session will be led by Dr. W. Timothy Garvey, MD, and will showcase findings from preclinical research. This opportunity highlights the potential of the drug candidate in aiding weight management strategies.

data.title: Why Did Visionary Holdings (GV) Stock Soar 36% After Hours
data.description: After announcing a $20 million investment cooperation agreement with Qingdao Xihai Rongke, Toronto-based company Visionary Holdings (NASDAQ: GV) saw its shares rise by 35.8% to $0.28 in after-hours trading on Wednesday. Qingdao Xihai Rongke will be a key partner and investor in the joint anti-aging project based in China. The agreement includes the establishment of a project company to launch the "GV China Anti-Aging Research Center" and a series of "GV Anti-Aging Health Management Centers," initially targeting the Shandong market. The funding will be distributed in three installments: 30%, 40%, and 30% based on agreed-upon milestones. This strategic move aims to drive profitability and growth in the anti-aging and healthcare segment. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Boeing-built Space Launch System Core Stage Powers First Crewed Artemis Mission around the Moon
data.description: The recent Artemis II mission by NASA has set the stage for future moon landings. The mission saw the launch of NASA's Space Launch System rocket at 6:35 p.m. ET from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida on April 1, 2026. This rocket, featuring a core stage built by Boeing, aims to pave the way for upcoming missions. At eight and a half minutes into the launch, significant progress was made toward further exploration. This development highlights the advancements in space exploration and the potential for future missions to the moon.

data.title: Court rejects lawsuit against ban on church endorsements. IRS settlement allows churches to endorse candidates, surprising conservatives.
data.description: Conservatives had anticipated a win in the case as the I.R.S. reached a settlement permitting churches to express endorsement for candidates.
